I Ignored Monorepos for Years. Here’s Everything I Wish Someone Had Told Me.Google has run almost its entire company out of a single repository for twenty years. Once you understand why, you’ll never look at your folder structure the same way.
Say the word “monorepo” in a standup and watch the room split.
One person nods like a battle-scarred veteran. Another quietly opens a new tab and Googles it. A third confidently confuses it with “monolith” and burns the next ten minutes of everyone’s life.
I was the third person for way too long.
So here’s the honest, no-jargon version I wish someone had handed me years ago — what a monorepo actually is, why the biggest companies on earth swear by them, and the traps nobody warns you about until you’re already stuck in one.
Grab a coffee. This one’s worth it.
The whole idea in one sentence
A monorepo is one big repository that holds a bunch of separate projects.
That’s it. That’s the tweet.
Picture a company with a backend, an Android app, an iOS app, a website, and a shared UI library. Normally, each of those gets its own repo — five islands, five histories, five headaches. In a monorepo, they all move in together. Same…
